name: Connecticut Dietitian Certification
description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Connecticut - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Department of Public Health — Dietitian-Nutritionist Certification

address.description:
Connecticut Department of Public Health
Practitioner Licensing and Investigations Section
410 Capitol Ave., MS #12HLS
P.O. Box 340308
Hartford, CT 06134-0308

renewalCycle: Annual + CDR 75 CPEUs per 5 years (no state-level CE mandate)

requiredDocuments.name: CDR Registration Verification
requiredDocuments.description: Must be sent directly from CDR to the Connecticut DPH — self-reported copies are not accepted.

requiredDocuments.name: Completed eLicense Application
requiredDocuments.description: Submitted through the Connecticut eLicense online portal with the $190 fee.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Connecticut dietitian certification cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The initial certification fee is $190 through the eLicense portal. Annual renewal is $105. There is no state-mandated continuing education beyond maintaining your CDR registration.

mainEntity.name: Does Connecticut require state-level continuing education?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. Connecticut does not mandate state-specific continuing education for annual certification renewal. However, you must maintain active CDR registration, which requires 75 CPEUs every five years, including at least one CPEU in ethics or health equity.

mainEntity.name: Can Connecticut CDNs enroll in Medicaid?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Effective April 1, 2025, certified dietitian-nutritionists can enroll in Connecticut's Medicaid program (CMAP) and HUSKY Health. Reimbursement for services began on July 1, 2025.

mainEntity.name: Does Connecticut have reciprocity for dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Connecticut. To work in Connecticut, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Connecticut?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Connecticut while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.
